rxn_class_summary: Copper-electrocatalyzed vicinal 1,2-bis(difluoromethylation) of alkenes using Zn(CF2H)2(DMPU)2 as a dichotomous CF2H radical and anion source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of Zn(CF2H)2(DMPU)2 (Eonset = 0.25 V vs Fc/Fc+) generates a CF2H radical; a second anodic oxidation (Eonset = 0.20 V) converts alkyl-Cu(II)(CF2H) to alkyl-Cu(III)(CF2H)X intermediate; cathode reduces Zn(II) to Zn(0) (Eonset = -1.8 V) to balance charge

key_intermediates.name: secondary benzylic alkyl radical from alkene addition (11)
alkyl-Cu(III)(CF2H)(X) complex (14, X = OTf or ClO4)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes Zn(CF2H)2(DMPU)2 to release a CF2H radical that adds to the alkene terminus, giving a secondary benzylic radical; this radical is captured by an in-situ-formed Cu(II)-CF2H species to form an alkyl-Cu(III)(CF2H)(X) intermediate that undergoes reductive elimination to install the second CF2H group. The resulting Cu(I)-X is re-transmetalated by Zn(CF2H)2(DMPU)2 (the resulting Cu(I)-CF2H is reoxidized anodically to Cu(II)-CF2H), while at the cathode Zn(II) is reduced to Zn(0).
rate_determining_step: Reductive elimination from alkyl-Cu(III)(CF2H)(X) intermediate (TS-RE, ΔG‡ ≈ 18.18 kcal/mol from DFT); radical combination with Cu(II) is barrierless

probe_reagents.name: vinylcyclopropane radical clock (substrate 4)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 6 obtained as major product (42%) vs cyclopropane-retained 7 (8%), confirming benzylic radical intermediate (k ~ 10^8 s^-1)

probe_reagents.name: unactivated terminal alkene 8
probe_reagents.observation: Gave bis(difluoromethylation) product 9 in 42%, ruling out direct alkene oxidation pathway (path B) since unactivated alkene has higher oxidation potential

probe_reagents.name: Zn(SO2CF2H)2
probe_reagents.observation: Conventional radical CF2H source — gave only hydroxydifluoromethylated 3a' (40%), no vicinal bis product, showing need for dichotomous reagent

probe_reagents.name: no Cu catalyst (control)
probe_reagents.observation: Only 3a' (28%); no vicinal bis-CF2H — Cu essential for second CF2H delivery

probe_reagents.name: Zn(CF3)2(DMPU)2 / Zn(C3F7)2(DMPU)2 / ZnEt2
probe_reagents.observation: None gave vicinal bis(alkylation), highlighting unique dual role of Zn(CF2H)2(DMPU)2

notes: Alternative mechanism considered: direct anodic oxidation of alkene to radical cation followed by nucleophilic CF2H addition (path B) — disfavored because (i) unactivated alkene 8 still reacts, (ii) Ep of alkene (2.2 V) ≫ Ep of 2 (0.75 V). Off-pathway: in absence of Cu, benzylic radical is further oxidized to carbocation and trapped by trace water to give hydroxydifluoromethyl product 3a' (radical-polar crossover). DFT shows alkyl-Cu(III)(CF2H)(X) with X = ClO4/OTf has ΔΔG‡ 8–10 kcal/mol lower than bis-CF2H Cu(III), explaining role of counteranion. 19F NMR verified Cu-CF2H intermediates 13 and 16. Cathodic event is Zn(II)→Zn(0) deposition confirmed by EDX.
